title: cytisus leucanthus subsp. albus
intro: Cytisus leucanthus subsp. albus, also known as Rock White Broom or White Broom, is a sub-species of the Cytisus leucanthus plant, which is a type of flowering legume native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cytisus leucanthus subsp. albus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Cytisus leucanthus subsp. albus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
